summary: Implemented and isolated-tested a replay-resistant exact-byte materialization executor boundary without mutating canonical queue state.
observed: Execution requires a ready authority-bound plan, unchanged repository head, exact canonical byte digest, and an injected writer.
A committed request digest in the replay ledger blocks a second execution.
Five deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime; repository CI execution remains unobserved.
inferred: The gap between authorization and controlled mutation is closed at the reusable module level, but not yet at protected workflow provenance level.
proposed: Add a protected workflow that runs the committed authority and executor tests, authenticates actor/workflow identity, invokes a single-path writer, and appends the returned receipt to a replay ledger.
superseded: Authorization may directly imply mutation without a second head and byte check.
A successful authorization request can be reused after a committed mutation.
unresolved: Actor and workflow identity are not cryptographically authenticated.
Repository CI has not been observed executing the new tests.
privacy_boundary: Public repository artifacts and synthetic fixtures only; private chat, health data, credentials, identifying information, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
next_action: Wire authority and executor tests into protected CI, define an append-only receipt ledger schema, and connect a writer that can replace only operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json after rechecking expected head and exact bytes.
